The **2019 [European Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/European_Figure_Skating_Championships "European Figure Skating Championships")** took place in [Minsk](/wiki/Minsk "Minsk"), [Belarus](/wiki/Belarus "Belarus").[[1]](#cite_note-ISU11209-1) Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, ladies' singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pairs](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dancing](/wiki/Ice_dancing "Ice dancing").

## Records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2019_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Records")]

For the all-time highest scores, see [list of highest scores in figure skating](/wiki/List_of_highest_scores_in_figure_skating "List of highest scores in figure skating").

The following new [ISU best scores](/wiki/List_of_highest_scores_in_figure_skating#Progression_of_men's_highest_scores "List of highest scores in figure skating") were set during this competition:

| Total score | **225.66** | [[3]](#cite_note-isu-progress-P-TO-3) |
| [Ice dance](#Ice_dancing) | Rhythm dance | [France](/wiki/France "France") [Gabriella Papadakis](/wiki/Gabriella_Papadakis "Gabriella Papadakis") / [Guillaume Cizeron](/wiki/Guillaume_Cizeron "Guillaume Cizeron") | **84.79** | 25 January 2019 | [[4]](#cite_note-isu-progress-D-RD-4) |

## Qualification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2019_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Qualification")]

Skaters were eligible for the event if they represent a European [member nation](/wiki/International_figure_skating "International figure skating") of the [International Skating Union](/wiki/International_Skating_Union "International Skating Union") and have reached the age of 15 before 1 July 2018, in their place of birth. The corresponding competition for non-European skaters was the [2019 Four Continents Championships](/wiki/2019_Four_Continents_Figure_Skating_Championships "2019 Four Continents Figure Skating Championships"). National associations selected their entries according to their own criteria, but the ISU mandated that their selections achieve a minimum technical elements score (TES) at an international event prior to the European Championships.

### Minimum TES

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2019_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Minimum TES")]

The ISU stipulates that the minimum scores must be achieved at an ISU-recognized senior international competition in the ongoing or preceding season, no later than 21 days before the first official practice day.

| Minimum technical scores (TES) | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Discipline | [SP](/wiki/Short_program_(figure_skating) "Short program (figure skating)") / [RD](/wiki/Rhythm_dance "Rhythm dance") | [FS](/wiki/Free_skating "Free skating") / [FD](/wiki/Free_dance_(figure_skating) "Free dance (figure skating)") |
| Men | 28.00 | 46.00 |
| Ladies | 23.00 | 40.00 |
| Pairs | 25.00 | 42.00 |
| Ice dance | 26.00 | 42.00 |
| SP and FS scores may be attained at different events. | | |

### Number of entries per discipline

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2019_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Number of entries per discipline")]

Based on the results of the [2018 European Championships](/wiki/2018_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2018 European Figure Skating Championships"), the ISU allows each country one to three entries per discipline.
